input.headline: NASA unveils close-up pictures of interstellar comet 3I/Atlas

raw.headline: NASA unveils close-up pictures of interstellar comet 3I/Atlas

winner: NASA finally gave us a clear look at Comet Atlas—turns out it’s just a space rock with poor hygiene and no sense of direction.

candidates.text: Why did the interstellar comet break up with its binary? It said, "You're out of this *world*—I need someone more *galactic*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay on 'world' and 'galactic,' and ties to the comet's interstellar nature. However, it lacks specificity about 3I/Atlas and doesn't fully leverage the context of NASA's announcement."}

candidates.text: Why did the interstellar comet 3I/Atlas get a speeding ticket? NASA said its tail was blocking the cosmic traffic lane!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a specific reference to the comet, and includes a playful twist with cosmic traffic lanes. However, it's slightly generic in its humor setup and could be more surprising."}

candidates.text: NASA finally gave us a clear look at Comet Atlas—turns out it’s just a space rock with poor hygiene and no sense of direction.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative personification of the comet with humor and specificity, while maintaining brevity and natural English."}

candidates.text: Why did the interstellar comet 3I/Atlas get a ticket? Because it was caught speeding through the solar system!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat generic and relies on a common pun (speeding), but it does tie in the comet's interstellar nature with a humorous twist."}

candidates.text: NASA just sent back some photos of that new comet, 3I/Atlas—it’s moving pretty fast, I guess.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 2.0, "reason": "Generic and lacks surprise or humor; simply restates the headline without adding any comedic element."}
